data.title: Trump Threatens 'Hell' — Iran Says It's All Going To Plan
data.description:
• USO shares are moving. See the chart and price action here. 

In tying further strikes directly to Iran's willingness to restore tanker traffic through the chokepoint, Trump has weaponized the calendar for traders, forcing them to price binary outcomes (ceasefire or escalation) around his timetable.

Iran's answer — that "the Strait of Hormuz will never return to its previous/former state, especially for America and Israel" and that the IRGC Navy is completing preparations for a "new order in the Persian Gulf" — signals that Tehran does not intend to simply roll back the clock even if a ceasefire framework emerges. 

The message is that Hormuz is now a permanent pressure point in Iran's deterrence toolkit, not just a temporary bargaining chip to be surrendered in exchange for sanctions relief or a pause in U.S.–Israeli strikes.

Impacts on Oil 

For oil, this turns what might have been treated as a transitory supply interruption into a structural risk premium. 

With roughly 20% of global crude and LNG flows tied to Hormuz, any credible suggestion that U.S. and Israeli‑linked traffic will face chronic disruption or higher tolls supports a higher floor for Brent and WTI, even if outright volumes resume over time. 

Volatility is likely to remain elevated as each new Trump post or IRGC statement reshapes the probabilities investors assign to an escalation that could damage fields, export terminals or naval assets.

Broader Impacts

State Street's recent analysis argues the war will ultimately prove more damaging than the 2025 tariff shock as both Washington and Tehran are now publicly locking in hard red lines.

Markets are pricing in fatter risk premiums for energy, shipping and insurance, as well as persistent margin pressure for energy‑intensive sectors. 

Image: Shutterstock AI Generator

data.title: Seagate Stock Soars To Fresh Highs: What To Watch
data.description:
Seagate Technology Holdings PLC (NASDAQ:STX) stock is pushing above key resistance as shares surge Monday, testing breakout territory after a sharp move higher.

  • Seagate Technology stock is at critical resistance. What’s behind STX new highs?

STXBreakout Test Underway

Seagate is up around 6.5% on Monday, reaching new highs and marking a potential breakout point. A sustained move above this area could signal continued upside, while rejection may reinforce near-term consolidation after the recent run.

Seagate stock has gained more than 580% over the past year as investors increasingly view it as a key AI infrastructure play, with hyperscale data centers driving strong demand for its high-capacity enterprise hard drives used for large-scale data storage and archiving.

With the strong gains over the past year, momentum indicators show some caution. The relative strength index previously entered overbought territory in February, which can coincide with strong upward momentum but may also increase the likelihood of sharper pullbacks.

With a breakout to new highs currently ongoing, resistance levels are in the rear view mirror. On the downside, $391.00 stands as a key support level, representing a prior demand zone where buyers have previously stepped in.

A hold above resistance could reinforce bullish momentum, while a move back toward support may test the strength of the current trend.

STX Shares Rise Monday

STX Price Action: At the time of writing, Seagate shares are trading 6.33% higher at $456.52, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: FDA Flags Anktiva Claims While ImmunityBio Pushes Regulatory Fixes
data.description:
The FDA issued a warning to ImmunityBio, alleging that the company made unsupported claims that Anktiva could cure or prevent cancer and suggested effectiveness beyond its approved use, including treatment for multiple cancers and use as a standalone therapy.

ImmunityBio subsequently initiated a comprehensive review of all promotional materials and external communications claims with its legal and regulatory teams.

The actions included mandatory executive training, expanded Promotional Review Committee protocols, and the engagement of external regulatory counsel to audit future high-visibility communications.

ImmunityBio has submitted a comprehensive response to the FDA’s Office of Prescription Drug Promotion (OPDP) regarding concerns about a podcast and a television advertisement.

The company confirmed that the television ad was never aired and is implementing corrective actions, including enhanced internal protocols and mandatory executive training.

ImmunityBio Growth Driven By Anktiva Sales Momentum

In March, the National Comprehensive Cancer Network (NCCN) updated its Clinical Practice Guidelines in Oncology for Bladder Cancer.

The agency included Anktiva in combination with Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) for BCG-unresponsive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer (NMIBC) with papillary-only disease.

ImmunityBio reported a remarkable year-over-year increase in revenue, driven by the sales of Anktiva.

Sales jumped from $7.56 million to $38.29 million, beating the consensus of $37.02 million.

The company reported a 750% unit sales volume increase for Anktiva.

IBRX Stock Price Activity: ImmunityBio shares were up 0.14% at $7.31 during premarket trading on Monday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: If You Invested $1000 In STMicroelectronics Stock 10 Years Ago, You Would Have This Much Today
data.description:
STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) has outperformed the market over the past 10 years by 8.41% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 20.72%. Currently, STMicroelectronics has a market capitalization of $30.69 billion.

Buying $1000 In STM: If an investor had bought $1000 of STM stock 10 years ago, it would be worth $6,420.68 today based on a price of $34.53 for STM at the time of writing.

STMicroelectronics's Performance Over Last 10 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

data.title: Bitcoin Closes In On $70,000: Is A Counter-Trend Rally Coming?
data.description:
Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) is consolidating close to $70,000, even as broader markets, particularly the S&P 500, show signs of breakdown.

Bitcoin Consolidating But Stronger Than Equities

In an Apr.4 podcast, analysts Cred and DonAlt note that Bitcoin is beginning to display relative strength compared to equities, suggesting a potential shift in market dynamics.

From a technical standpoint, the current range between $57,000 and $61,000 is seen as fair value. Holding the $58,000–$60,000 zone is critical for bulls; losing it could trigger a sharp drop toward $40,000.

On the upside, a weekly close above $71,000 would signal renewed bullish momentum, while reclaiming $82,000, a key resistance level, could open the door to new highs.

A strong bullish setup would emerge if Bitcoin held support while SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE:SPY) continue to decline, indicating decoupling.

Altcoins Under Pressure

Analysts Don and Cred noted that the altcoin market remains under pressure.

Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) has broken below its key $120 support and could face a steep decline toward $40 if it fails to recover.

Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) is holding near $2,000 and maintaining key support levels but lacks strong upward momentum.

The broader trend suggests a shift away from speculative meme coins, with declining retail interest like the NFT collapse in the previous cycle.

Future market liquidity is expected to favor projects with real utility and revenue generation rather than hype-driven assets.

Image: Shutterstock
